title: Buyer Rescue Workflow
short: A simple buyer-support issue becomes a proof-backed workflow improvement.
problem: A buyer cannot access a download, cannot find the right file, or receives a confusing reply. Ordinary support automation may answer, but it does not leave a reusable proof artifact that improves future support work.
mission: Improve a buyer-support workflow from an incomplete response into a clear rescue path with access classification, refund/access boundary, escalation flag, proof note, and reusable support playbook.
why: The first proof must be instantly understandable. Support is the front door of trust: if the system can rescue a buyer and preserve the improvement, it begins converting service into reusable capability.
goals: preserve privacy while creating public trust
metrics: specific next step and escalation rule
agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates the support proof mission: sponsor funds the improvement, builder claims with a bond, reviewer validates evidence, and approved work records credential and reputation.
rsi: The support workflow may influence future support only after the reviewer validates the before/after improvement and the Selection Gate grants scoped upgrade rights.
evidence: Red-team or challenge report where applicable
status: Experimental public proof-card dossier / evidence-docket template
plain_english: A buyer support issue becomes a reusable, reviewer-validated workflow improvement.

Private data, sensitive workflows, security details, buyer data, and confidential operational context.

Only artifacts that survive proof, evaluation, review, and selection may shape future work.

AGIALPHA coordinates mission budgets, bonds, settlement, proof-card actions, credentials, and reputation-linked access.

title: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room

plans: reuse accepted answer in future procurement

title: Sovereign AI Procurement Control Tower
short: A complex AI vendor-evaluation process becomes a governed proof mission portfolio.

mission: Build a procurement control tower that decomposes AI-adoption questions into proof missions, routes them to specialist agents, validates evidence, and produces a public-safe decision dossier.
why: This shifts GoalOS from a product into a decision infrastructure layer for AI adoption. It becomes the control plane for institutional AI procurement.
goals: make proof the basis for procurement
agialpha: AGIALPHA funds and coordinates the procurement proof mission portfolio, including builder, reviewer, red-team, proof-card, credential, and reputation actions.
rsi: Procurement criteria and rubrics evolve only when validated decisions improve future evaluations without weakening risk controls.

title: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine
short: The system accumulates capabilities, evidence, and economic value over time.
problem: Most AI work is episodic. It answers, acts, and disappears. A persistent intelligence system must remember what worked, under which constraints, and how it should influence future work.
mission: Demonstrate an experimental framework where GoalOS sets persistent aims, AGIALPHA coordinates proof work, Evidence Dockets preserve results, and selected artifacts accumulate into future operating context.
why: This is the transition from task automation to institutional intelligence. It defines how a system can compound capability rather than merely produce outputs.
plans: reuse selected artifact in next mission
agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates recurring proof work, settlement, credentialing, and reputation across a persistent mission history.
rsi: Recursive self-improvement becomes a controlled memory mechanism: selected artifacts may influence the next run; rejected artifacts cannot.
evidence: Red-team or challenge report where applicable
status: Experimental public proof-card dossier / evidence-docket template
